steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The word 'but' signals a contrast: the habitat explanation for the species' absence is being challenged by the fact that the region is 'notoriously inaccessible.' The correct answer must explain how inaccessibility undermines the habitat claim.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘*M. knippeliana* may have been overlooked in eastern and western Coahuila because of its similarity to another species.’: species confusion is unrelated to the region's inaccessibility. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘the current methods of collecting and tracking *Mammillaria* species throughout Mexico may cause an overestimation of the number of species in this genus.’: this is about overestimation of species counts due to collection methods across Mexico — it has nothing to do with the inaccessibility of Coahuila specifically. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘the perceived absence of *Mammillaria* in eastern and western Coahuila may be due to insufficient exploration of the region.’: directly connects inaccessibility to the possibility that the species simply hasn't been found yet, contrasting the habitat explanation. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘the dense vegetation and high annual precipitation levels in eastern and western Coahuila impede the ability of *Mammillaria* species to survive.’: this restates the original habitat-suitability hypothesis that the 'but' clause is meant to challenge. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
